zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Baskerville於1284年出生在英國賀福爾易亞迪斯利時，她的父親Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae16歲，母親Sybella Corbett9歲。她和Sir John Scudmore至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Baskerville于1284年出生在英国賀福爾易亞迪斯利时，她的父亲Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae16岁，母亲Sybella Corbett9岁。她和Sir John Scudmore至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Baskerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Bois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Baskerville was born in 1284, in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, her father, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, was 16 and her mother, Sybella Corbett, was 9. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Sir John Scudmore.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Baskerville est née en 1284, à Eardisley, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Sybella Corbett, avait 9 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Sir John Scudmore.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Baskerville 1284 in Eardisley, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae 16 und ihre Mutter Sybella Corbett 9. Sie hatte mit Sir John Scudmore mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Baskerville è nata il nel 1284 nel Eardisley, Herefordshire, England, sua padre, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Sybella Corbett, aveva 9 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Sir John Scudmore.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Baskerville(이)가 1284년 영국 허퍼드실르 에르디슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybella Corbett(은)는 9세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Scudmore(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Baskerville nasceu em 1284, em Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Sybella Corbett, tinha 9 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Sir John Scudmore.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Baskerville родилась в 1284 г. в местности эардисли, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybella Corbett, 9. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Sir John Scudmore.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Baskerville nació en 1284, en Eardisley, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter de Baskerville Lord of Eardisley and Lord of Combae, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Sybella Corbett, tenía 9 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Sir John Scudmore.
